title: गायत्री हृदयम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री गायत्री हृदयम् ॥

ॐ भूर्भुवः स्वः तत्सवितुर्वरेण्यं
भर्गो देवस्य धीमहि। धियो यो नः प्रचोदयात्॥

आयातु वरदा देवी अक्षरं ब्रह्मसम्मितम्।
गायत्रीं छन्दसां माता ब्रह्मयोनिं नमाम्यहम्॥ १॥

मुक्ताविद्रुमहेमनीलधवलच्छायैर्मुखैस्त्रीक्षणैः
युक्तामिन्दुनिबद्धरत्नमुकुटां तत्त्वार्थवर्णात्मिकाम्।
गायत्रीं वरदाभयाङ्कुशकशाशूलं कपालं गदां
शङ्खं चक्रमथारविन्दयुगलं हस्तैर्वहन्तीं भजे॥ २॥

गायत्री चैव सावित्री च सरस्वती च।
त्रयी विद्या परा शक्तिः एषा गायत्री वेदमाता॥ ३॥

गायत्र्या हृदयं वक्ष्ये सर्वकामफलप्रदम्।
य इदं श्रृणुयान्नित्यं पठेद्वा श्रद्धयान्वितः।
सर्वपापविनिर्मुक्तः परं ब्रह्माधिगच्छति॥ ४॥

प्रातः सन्ध्यां समासाद्य गायत्रीमुपतिष्ठति।
सायं सन्ध्यां समासाद्य गायत्रीमुपतिष्ठति।
मध्याह्ने तु समासाद्य गायत्रीमुपतिष्ठति॥ ५॥

सर्वव्याधिविनिर्मुक्तः सर्वरोगविवर्जितः।
गायत्रीहृदयं जप्त्वा सर्वान्कामानवाप्नुयात्॥ ६॥

ब्रह्मणोऽस्य मुखं गायत्री
ऋषिः सविता देवता।
अग्निर्मुखं वायुर्हृदयं
सूर्य आत्मा चन्द्रमा मनः॥ ७॥

॥ इति श्री गायत्री हृदयम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Gayatri Hridayam ||

Om Bhur Bhuvah Svah Tat Savitur Varenyam
Bhargo Devasya Dhimahi | Dhiyo Yo Nah Prachodayat ||

Aayatu Varada Devi Aksharam Brahmasammitam |
Gayatrim Chhandasam Mata Brahmayonim Namamy Aham || 1 ||

Mukta Vidruma Hema Nila Dhavala Chhayair Mukhais Trikshanai
Yuktam Indu Nibaddha Ratna Mukutam Tattvartha Varnatmikam |
Gayatrim Varada Abhaya Ankusha Kasha Shulam Kapalam Gadam
Shankham Chakram Atha Aravinda Yugalam Hastair Vahantim Bhaje || 2 ||

Gayatri Chaiva Savitri Cha Sarasvati Cha |
Trayi Vidya Para Shaktih Esha Gayatri Vedamata || 3 ||

Gayatrya Hridayam Vakshye Sarva Kama Phala Pradam |
Ya Idam Shrinuyan Nityam Pathed Va Shraddhaya Anvitah |
Sarva Papa Vinirmuktah Param Brahmadhigachchhati || 4 ||

Pratah Sandhyam Samasadya Gayatrim Upatishtati |
Sayam Sandhyam Samasadya Gayatrim Upatishtati |
Madhyahne Tu Samasadya Gayatrim Upatishtati || 5 ||

Sarva Vyadhi Vinirmuktah Sarva Roga Vivarjitah |
Gayatri Hridayam Japtva Sarvan Kaman Avapnuyat || 6 ||

Brahmano'sya Mukham Gayatri
Rishih Savita Devata |
Agni Mukham Vayur Hridayam
Surya Atma Chandrama Manah || 7 ||

|| Iti Shri Gayatri Hridayam Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
ॐ भूर्भुवः स्वः — उस सविता देव के श्रेष्ठ तेज का हम ध्यान करें, जो हमारी बुद्धि को प्रेरित करे।

वरदायिनी देवी गायत्री आएं — जो अक्षर ब्रह्म समान हैं, छन्दों की माता हैं, ब्रह्मयोनि हैं — उन्हें नमस्कार।

मुक्ता, विद्रुम, हेम, नील, श्वेत छाया वाले मुखों से युक्त, त्रिनेत्रा, चन्द्रमणिमुकुटधारिणी, तत्त्वार्थवर्णात्मिका गायत्री — वरद, अभय, अंकुश, कशा, शूल, कपाल, गदा, शंख, चक्र और कमलयुगल हस्तों में धारण करने वाली को भजता हूँ।

गायत्री, सावित्री और सरस्वती — त्रयी विद्या, पराशक्ति, वेदमाता।

गायत्री के इस हृदय (सार) को सुनने या श्रद्धापूर्वक पढ़ने वाला सर्वपापमुक्त होकर परब्रह्म को प्राप्त करता है। प्रातः, सायं और मध्याह्न सन्ध्या में गायत्री का उपासन करें। गायत्री हृदय जपने से सर्वव्याधिमुक्त होकर सर्वकाम प्राप्त होते हैं।
benefits: Heart (essence) of Gayatri Mantra with complete meditation and description
Describes Gayatri Devi's cosmic form with ten hands and five faces
Invokes all desires and removes all sins when recited with faith
Essential for Sandhya Vandana practitioners seeking deeper understanding
Frees from all diseases and leads to Brahma realization
description: The Gayatri Hridayam, known in Sanskrit as गायत्री हृदयम्, is a heart-essence prayer in praise of Goddess Gayatri. This revered text originates from Atharvaveda Parishishta. Devotees recite this prayer to grant all desires and remove all sins when recited with faith and heart (essence) of Gayatri Mantra with complete meditation and description. It is also said to free from all diseases and leads to Brahma realization. This sacred text is ideally recited on Daily and during Gayatri Jayanti and Guru Purnima for enhanced blessings. With 7 verses and a reading time of about 4 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Gayatri Hridayam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Gayatri Hridayam and where is it preserved?
seoFaqs.answer: The Gayatri Hridayam (गायत्री हृदयम्) is a hridaya-stotra — a 'heart-essence hymn' — that unfolds the inner meaning, iconography and meditative form of Devi Gayatri (Veda Mata, the Mother of all Vedas) in seven concentrated verses. The text is preserved in the Atharvaveda Parishishta (the supplementary Vedic literature attached to the Atharvaveda) and is recited as part of the deeper, dhyana-oriented dimension of Sandhya Vandana. It opens with the mula Gayatri Mantra itself (Om Bhur Bhuvah Svah, Tat Savitur Varenyam, Bhargo Devasya Dhimahi, Dhiyo Yo Nah Prachodayat), then proceeds to invoke the Devi as the consort of Brahma (Brahma-yoni), the Mother of all chhandas, and the supreme Brahma-sammita Akshara — equal to Brahman itself. (Sources: Atharvaveda Parishishta; Vedic upasana tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: How does the Gayatri Hridayam describe the form of Devi Gayatri?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 2 of the Gayatri Hridayam paints one of the most detailed and beautiful iconographic descriptions of Devi Gayatri in the entire Vedic literature: Mukta-vidruma-hema-nila-dhavala-chchhayair-mukhais-trikshanaih — 'with five faces of pearl-white, coral-red, golden-yellow, sapphire-blue and white hues, each face having three eyes'; Yuktam-indu-nibaddha-ratna-mukutam — 'crowned with a jewelled crown bound by the moon'; Tattvartha-varnatmikam — 'whose very letters embody the truths of all tattvas.' Her ten hands hold: varada (boon-giving mudra), abhaya (fearlessness mudra), ankusha (goad), kasha (whip), shula (trident), kapala (skull-cup), gada (mace), shankha (conch), chakra (discus) and aravinda-yugala (a pair of lotuses). This pancha-mukhi dasha-bhuja form represents the complete cosmic shakti underlying the Gayatri Mantra. (Sources: Gayatri Hridayam verse 2; Devi Bhagavata.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Gayatri called the Mother of all Vedas?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 3 of the Gayatri Hridayam declares — Gayatri chaiva Savitri cha Sarasvati cha, trayi vidya para shaktih esha Gayatri Vedamata — 'Gayatri, Savitri and Sarasvati: the three Vidyas, the supreme Shakti, this is Gayatri the Mother of the Vedas.' The Gayatri Mantra (Rig Veda 3.62.10) is considered the seed-mantra from which all four Vedas issued forth, its 24 syllables corresponding to the 24 essential principles of creation. The trinity of Gayatri (presiding over the morning sandhya, the rising sun, the Rigveda), Savitri (presiding over the noon sandhya, the zenith sun, the Yajurveda) and Sarasvati (presiding over the evening sandhya, the setting sun, the Samaveda) together constitute the integrated Vedamata — the cosmic Mother who births, sustains and ultimately dissolves all Vedic knowledge. The Atharvaveda is presided over by all three. (Sources: Gayatri Hridayam verse 3; Devi Bhagavata 12; Vedic tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: How does the Gayatri Hridayam structure cosmic correspondences?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 7 of the Gayatri Hridayam establishes a series of profound cosmic correspondences that situate the Gayatri at the centre of the entire universe of yajna: Brahmano'sya mukham Gayatri — 'the very mouth of this Veda is Gayatri'; Rishih Savita devata — 'the rishi is Savita, the deity is Savita'; Agnir-mukham — 'fire is the mouth (of yajna)'; Vayur-hridayam — 'wind is the heart'; Surya atma — 'the sun is the atman'; Chandrama manah — 'the moon is the mind.' These correspondences echo the Purusha Sukta (Rig Veda 10.90) and the Brihadaranyaka Upanishad's macrocosm-microcosm equations, revealing that the Gayatri is not merely a verbal mantra but the very structure of the cosmos in sound. To meditate on the Gayatri Hridayam is therefore to meditate on the entire cosmic order. (Sources: Gayatri Hridayam verse 7; Brihadaranyaka Upanishad; Purusha Sukta.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should the Gayatri Hridayam be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Gayatri Hridayam is most powerfully recited at the three sandhyas (pratah, madhyahna and sayam) — the daily junctures of dawn, noon and dusk that are the Gayatri's natural worship-times, as explicitly enjoined by verse 5 of the Hridayam itself. It is especially auspicious on Gayatri Jayanti (traditionally observed on Jyeshtha Shukla Ekadashi, the day Gayatri Mata revealed the Gayatri Mantra to Sage Vishvamitra); on every Sunday (Ravivar — Surya's day, since Savita is the devata); on Vasant Panchami; on Guru Purnima (when one's Brahmopadesha guru is honoured); during the Upanayana ceremony when the Gayatri is given to a brahmacharin; and continuously by Vedic students and brahmacharis. The recitation should be done after bath, facing the Sun if possible, with the sacred thread (yajnopavita) properly worn. (Sources: Sandhya Vandana paddhati; Vedic tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the benefits of reciting the Gayatri Hridayam?
seoFaqs.answer: The Gayatri Hridayam itself promises in verse 4 — Gayatrya hridayam vakshye sarva-kama-phala-pradam, ya idam shrinuyan-nityam pathed-va shraddhaya-anvitah, sarva-papa-vinirmuktah param brahmadhigachchhati: 'I shall declare the heart of Gayatri, which grants the fruit of all desires; one who hears or recites this daily with faith is freed from all sins and attains the Supreme Brahman.' Verse 6 adds — sarva-vyadhi-vinirmuktah sarva-roga-vivarjitah, Gayatri-hridayam japtva sarvan-kaman-avapnuyat: 'free from all diseases and ailments, by japa of Gayatri Hridayam one obtains all desires.' Traditional acharyas additionally extol: deepening of Sandhya practice through inner understanding of the mantra; cultivation of buddhi-sattva through Savita's pranava; awakening of Kundalini through the 24-syllable resonance; sharpening of intellect for Vedic study; and ultimately Brahma-sakshatkara. (Sources: Gayatri Hridayam Phalashruti.)
